title: Feature Request: Enhanced Charting Options for Project Progress Tracking

posts.author: monomono (Moe Nomura)
posts.cookedHtml:
Description

I would like to request improved charting capabilities within Monday.com, specifically the ability to create combination charts such as a bar chart (actual progress) combined with a line chart (planned progress). At the moment, Monday.com does not support these types of charts, which makes it difficult to properly visualize planned vs. actual progress directly within the platform.
Because of this limitation, I currently need to export data to Excel in order to create combo charts, which interrupts workflow continuity and prevents project progress tracking from being fully completed inside Monday.com.
Additionally, I would like more flexibility in selecting values for both the X‑axis and Y‑axis, as the current options are quite limited. I strongly feel that chart functionality should at least match the basic capabilities available in Excel so that users can create meaningful and accurate visualizations without using external tools.

What are you trying to achieve

I want to be able to visualize planned vs. actual progress within Monday.com without needing to export data to Excel. By enabling combo charts (e.g., bar + line) and providing more flexible axis options, I aim to manage all progress tracking—forecasting, monitoring deviations, and sharing status—with my team directly on Monday.com. This would streamline workflow, improve project visibility, and remove the need for external charting tools.
